title: Who what could stand up or defeat an Evangelion
display_title: Who what could stand up or defeat an Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Who/what could stand up or defeat an Evangelion?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 9:59 pm Since it's already been established that the Evangelions themselves are tough hombres, considering they have the potential to become gods, who or what do you think outside the Evangelion continuity/universe has the potential to possibly defeat any one of the Evas, or even hold their own against these biomechs? The Evangelions themselves have a pretty tough standing in the mecha community as being the most powerful weapons to exist, but they're not truly invincible. Who/what do you think has the potential to bring down an Eva (through direct or indirect means) and why?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Oct 26, 2012 10:09 pm Countless things, including moeblobs:

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Oct 26, 2012 10:30 pm A magical girl from the Madokaverse.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Oct 26, 2012 10:42 pm Nirvash ( Eureka Seven ) would probably have a good shot. Madara Uchiha ( Naruto ) and his Perfect Susano'o could definitely deal some damage to an Eva. Gurren Lagann's base form ( Tengen Toppa Gurren Lagann ) would be a formidable opponent; the larger ones could no doubt squash an Eva pretty handily. I hate to say it, but an Eva would run circles around Big O, ( The Big O ) and all of the other Bigs as well. I still really like the design of the mechs from that series, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 11:09 pm Isn't this question the reason why Super Robot Wars exists as a franchise? On a case by case basis: Empirical comparisons are difficult when entities, robots, et al., are defined and limited by the rules of their respective universes. Gurren Lagann makes Eva-01 looks like small potatoes, but the metaphysics in NGE are very different from those in TTGL, so it's all moot in the end.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Oct 26, 2012 11:21 pm Flash SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 27, 2012 3:17 am Oh, look, it's a "versus" thread. So let's pull out the good old /a/ favourites, like Demonbane; or, if we're looking for a truly broken and overkill suggestion, let's just turn things up to eleven with Sakura Kinomoto with the Sakura cards. The ease with which Bardiel operated suggests that even if an Evangelion might not register directly to a Jain node (depends on the detection as product vs person), any of the users of that or equivalent level viral utility fog tech from Neal Asher's Polity series would barely break a sweat. Going up the tech scale, any one capable of hacking reality directly to create and destroy matter at distance (the end-state of John Campbell's super-scientists, the Dawn Treader in Bear's Anvil of Stars , or even just Dr Manhattan). And as the Angels themselves showed, a high enough power telepath can do the job; so I would guess pretty much any significant person or menace from the Lensman series could do it from unawares; and anything second stage or up would work through or around AT fields to get at pilot and EVA both. And from a different continuity, I suspect that by the time Telzey Amberdon hit her seventeenth birthday, she would be like "You could be my puppet, but instead I think I'll just fix your psychic damage for you."

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Oct 27, 2012 6:29 am Reichu wrote: Isn't this question the reason why Super Robot Wars exists as a franchise?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:33 am A Star Destroyer.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Sat Oct 27, 2012 8:46 am (indirectly) Alucard post Schrodinger. Just kill the pilot xD or if the Eva is running off the dummy plug, teleport into the plug and destroy the mechanisms from the inside.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Sat Oct 27, 2012 9:05 am SYSTEM ∀-99 ∀ Gundam/CONCEPT-X 6-1-2 Turn X

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Oct 27, 2012 10:06 am ... Would Heero Yuy's Gundam be able to withstand these angels? With his fearless personality, he should be. 'course, Chang Wufei's Gundam would also help out.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Sat Oct 27, 2012 10:07 am tomrule123 wrote: ... Would Heero Yuy's Gundam be able to withstand these angels? With his fearless personality, he should be.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Oct 27, 2012 10:09 am Tankred wrote: Focused fire finished off both of his gundams.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sat Oct 27, 2012 10:41 am CJD wrote: Flash.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Oct 27, 2012 10:48 am I hate vs. threads, but I like Xard's answer the best so far. And Sentry is lame. I could come up with lamer things if I tried, but I'd have to work at it.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sat Oct 27, 2012 11:23 am tomrule123 wrote: ...Would Heero Yuy's Gundam be able to withstand these angels? With his fearless personality, he should be. 'course, Chang Wufei's Gundam would also help out.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Oct 27, 2012 11:24 am yeh vs threads blow and the range of possible answers is pretty much endless due to amount of ludicruously overpowering beings in fiction.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Oct 28, 2012 12:27 am Bagheera wrote: And Sentry is lame.

[]: AND METALGARURUMON! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Oct 28, 2012 2:31 am Xard wrote: he range of possible answers is pretty much endless

The Evange
